text.heading: Atiku, Haruna united, ready to tackle Nyako, says Elias
text.p: Former Adamawa State Commissioner for Finance, John Elias, has said that the relationship between former Vice President Atiku Abubakar and former Adamawa State Governor Boni Haruna is still cordial.

Elias said: "as far as I am concerned, the relationship between former Vice President Atiku Abubakar and Boni Haruna is still cordial. What I understand in their relationship is that Boni Haruna is only expressing displeasure over certain things that are happening in Atiku’s group. Atiku is now relating with people who were not favourably disposed to his political beliefs for Nigeria, and he Atiku Abubakar has given those people a kind of freedom to take crucial decisions on his political future."

He added that "Haruna is one of Atiku’s core loyalists, in fact the most loyal to Atiku right from 1998 till date, because what he (Boni) did during the crisis between Obasanjo and Atiku, nobody will ever do and I think Haruna deserves to be treated well, respected and heard since he has the grassroots support."

Continuing, Elias said: "Haruna is my leader and my political head, I don’t believe that the rumour about Boni leaving Atiku will materialise but what I believe is that once you are with somebody, there must be displeasure over certain things and there is room for reconciliation."

Elias described the Murtala Nyako administration a failure. In his words, "There is no comparison between our administration and Nyako’s administration because Nyako’s style of leadership is more sentimental towards widening the gap between the Fulani tribe and the rest which means the non-Fulani are not welcome in his government. The way he administers this state can’t be compared with Boni’s."

He said the Nyako administration believes in spending much and achieving little. "Let us take you to a particular project in Jimeta, the Yola–Numan road, from the PZ roundabout Mai-dokey to welcome to Yola is not more than 4km but they spend more than N3b on it. So, if you are talking about a 2km road contracted at N168m is the usual thing here in Adamawa. They do over invoicing and contract inflation. So, this administration is just on spending spree. This is not good for us," he said.

According to him, the Haruna administration, which got less from the Federation Account, achieved more because of its prudence. "Former Governor Boni Haruna never awarded contracts without money. That is why when Boni left office, he left very little liabilities, forget about what the Nyako administration is saying that Boni left over N22b liabilities. It is a lie. Let them publish it and show the world the facts. Boni Haruna should be praised for what he did, the way he carried everybody along, and massive development of Adamawa. The state secretariat today remains one of the best in Nigeria, the Government House also one of the best, workers salaries allowances, grants and entitlement were paid as at when due; more people went back to school, even the market women. He improved educational institutions in the state.

"I think with time, people will see and appreciate the Boni administration as one of the best in the Nigeria ’s democracy from 1999 to 2007. Governor Nyako with all the huge resources at his disposal has failed. There is much corruption in Adamawa State and the press has seen the dirty and crude scandals in Adamawa Government House under the nose of Murtala Nyako," Elias said.
